avoid: Replacing the full W@X GEMM with a custom kernel; cuBLAS is already highly optimized and unlikely to be beaten.
Hardcoding hidden dimensions or tensor sizes in the kernel.
Over-optimizing for a single shape (e.g., 3584) at the expense of others.
evidence: Correctness is perfect (max_abs_err=0.0, rel_l2_err=0.0) for all shapes.
Aggregate speedup is only 1.02x, with speedup decreasing as d increases: 1.04x at 3584, 1.025x at 4096, and 0.99x (slowdown) at 4608.
Custom kernel uses scalar accesses (vector_width=1) and fixed block_size=512, likely causing poor memory coalescing and bandwidth utilization.
Profile summary suggests bottleneck is rank16_update_or_memory_bandwidth and recommends vectorization (float4) and block size tuning.

recommended_actions: try float4 stores when d is divisible by 4
fall back to ATen if numerical error appears
Implement vectorized loads/stores (e.g., float4) to improve memory throughput, especially since d is divisible by 4 for all target shapes.
Tune block size per shape; smaller blocks may improve occupancy for larger d, or use a shape-aware dispatch to select optimal block size.
Use shared memory to cache tiles of X or intermediate results to reduce redundant global memory accesses.
Profile the custom kernel to determine if it is memory-bound or compute-bound, and adjust optimization strategy accordingly.
Consider falling back to cuBLAS for the rank-16 update at d=4608 if custom kernel cannot beat it after tuning.
